Issues.Description: NORMALIZER GK PORT IS TRANSLATED AS ROW_NUMBER() WHICH ALWAYS STARTS FROM 1 PER QUERY EXECUTION. THIS IS FUNCTIONALLY EQUIVALENT TO RESTART=YES IN INFORMATICA POWERCENTER. VERIFY GK VALUES IF USED AS A FOREIGN KEY IN DOWNSTREAM TARGETS.

Issues.FriendlyName: Normalizer GK port uses ROW_NUMBER — Reset=YES behavior not replicated.
Issues.Description: NORMALIZER RESET=YES IS NOT REPLICATED IN SNOWFLAKE DBT. IN INFORMATICA, RESET=YES RESTORES THE GK COUNTER TO ITS PRE-SESSION VALUE AT SESSION END. IN THE DBT TRANSLATION, ROW_NUMBER() ALWAYS STARTS FROM 1. VERIFY GK VALUES IF USED AS A FOREIGN KEY IN DOWNSTREAM TARGETS.

Issues.FriendlyName: Sorter Transformation Scope Transaction not modelled.
Issues.Description: SORTER TRANSFORMATION SCOPE IS SET TO TRANSACTION. TRANSACTION BOUNDARIES ARE NOT MODELLED IN SNOWFLAKE SQL. THE SORT IS APPLIED GLOBALLY ACROSS ALL INPUT ROWS. VERIFY THAT GLOBAL SORTING PRODUCES CORRECT RESULTS FOR THE DOWNSTREAM LOGIC.

Issues.FriendlyName: ROUND(date) converted to DATE_TRUNC which always truncates instead of rounding.
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA ROUND(DATE, '{0}') ROUNDS TO THE NEAREST DATE BOUNDARY (E.G. PAST THE MIDPOINT OF A MONTH ROUNDS UP TO NEXT MONTH). SNOWFLAKE DATE_TRUNC ALWAYS TRUNCATES TO THE START OF THE PERIOD. RESULTS MAY DIFFER FOR DATES PAST THE MIDPOINT.

Issues.FriendlyName: Informatica ROUND uses half-away-from-zero rounding which may differ from Snowflake banker's rounding on FLOAT types.
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA ROUND AND TO_INTEGER USE HALF-AWAY-FROM-ZERO ROUNDING (0.5 ROUNDS TO 1, -0.5 ROUNDS TO -1). SNOWFLAKE ROUND ON FLOAT TYPES USES BANKER'S ROUNDING (ROUND HALF TO EVEN). RESULTS MAY DIFFER FOR VALUES EXACTLY AT .5 BOUNDARIES. CONSIDER USING NUMERIC/DECIMAL TYPES FOR DETERMINISTIC ROUNDING.

Issues.FriendlyName: IS_DATE without format parameter depends on session date format.
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA IS_DATE WITHOUT A FORMAT PARAMETER RETURNS 0 FOR ALL VALUES (DEPENDS ON SESSION DATE FORMAT WHICH IS NOT MIGRATED). SNOWFLAKE TRY_TO_DATE WITHOUT FORMAT USES THE DATE_INPUT_FORMAT SESSION PARAMETER. RESULTS MAY DIFFER. SPECIFY AN EXPLICIT FORMAT STRING FOR DETERMINISTIC BEHAVIOR.

Issues.FriendlyName: Informatica IN case_flag parameter is not supported in Snowflake.
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA IN FUNCTION CASE_FLAG PARAMETER (CASE-INSENSITIVE COMPARISON) IS NOT SUPPORTED IN SNOWFLAKE. THE CASE_FLAG HAS BEEN REMOVED FROM THE TRANSLATED IN PREDICATE. SNOWFLAKE IN IS ALWAYS CASE-SENSITIVE. CONSIDER USING UPPER() OR LOWER() ON BOTH SIDES FOR CASE-INSENSITIVE COMPARISONS.

Issues.FriendlyName: SESSSTARTTIME converted to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() which may differ in long-running sessions.
Issues.Description: SESSSTARTTIME CONVERTED TO CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(). INFORMATICA SESSSTARTTIME RETURNS A STATIC TIMESTAMP CAPTURED AT SESSION INITIALIZATION. SNOWFLAKE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() RETURNS THE CURRENT TIME AT EACH ROW EVALUATION. RESULTS MAY DIFFER IN LONG-RUNNING SESSIONS.

Issues.FriendlyName: Sequence Generator row ordering is non-deterministic and state is not persisted.
Issues.Description: THE SEQUENCE GENERATOR IS TRANSLATED TO ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY 1). ROW ORDERING IS NON-DETERMINISTIC (VALUES MAY BE ASSIGNED TO DIFFERENT ROWS BETWEEN RUNS) AND SEQUENCE STATE IS NOT PERSISTED ACROSS DBT EXECUTIONS (ALWAYS RESTARTS FROM START VALUE). ADD A DETERMINISTIC ORDER BY IF STABLE SEQUENCE ASSIGNMENT IS REQUIRED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Sequence Generator Increment By value is not 1 — ROW_NUMBER() always increments by 1.
Issues.Description: THE SEQUENCE GENERATOR USES INCREMENT BY = {0} BUT THE TRANSLATED ROW_NUMBER() ALWAYS INCREMENTS BY 1. TO REPLICATE THE ORIGINAL SEQUENCE, REPLACE THE EXPRESSION WITH: (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY 1) - 1) * {0} + START_VALUE.

Issues.FriendlyName: Sequence Generator CURRVAL port is not supported.
Issues.Description: THE SEQUENCE GENERATOR CURRVAL PORT IS NOT SUPPORTED IN THE DBT TRANSLATION. CURRVAL RETURNS THE LAST GENERATED NEXTVAL VALUE. IF CURRVAL IS CONNECTED TO A DOWNSTREAM TRANSFORMATION, THE CORRESPONDING COLUMN WILL BE MISSING IN THE OUTPUT.

Issues.FriendlyName: Sequence Generator Cycle option is not supported.
Issues.Description: THE SEQUENCE GENERATOR HAS CYCLE ENABLED BUT THE TRANSLATED ROW_NUMBER() DOES NOT CYCLE. WHEN THE ORIGINAL SEQUENCE WOULD WRAP AROUND TO THE START VALUE, THE TRANSLATED SEQUENCE CONTINUES INCREMENTING. MANUAL ADJUSTMENT IS REQUIRED IF CYCLING BEHAVIOR IS NEEDED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Sequence Generator Start Value exceeds integer range.
Issues.Description: THE SEQUENCE GENERATOR START VALUE {0} PRODUCES AN OFFSET THAT EXCEEDS THE INTEGER RANGE SUPPORTED BY THE SQL LITERAL. THE GENERATED OFFSET HAS BEEN CLAMPED. MANUAL ADJUSTMENT OF THE START VALUE OFFSET IS REQUIRED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Update Strategy was overriden by session settings.
Issues.Description: UPDATE STRATEGY WAS OVERRIDEN BY SESSION SETTINGS.

Issues.FriendlyName: The update strategy logic was moved to the target model.
Issues.Description: THE UPDATE STRATEGY LOGIC WAS MOVED TO THE TARGET MODEL.

Issues.FriendlyName: Mapping has multiple session overrides of its update strategy.
Issues.Description: MAPPING HAS MULTIPLE SESSION OVERRIDES OF ITS UPDATE STRATEGY.

Issues.FriendlyName: Update strategy not directly connected to target transformation requires manual changes.
Issues.Description: UPDATE STRATEGY NOT CONNECTED TO TARGET TRANSFORMATION REQUIRES MANUAL CHANGES.

Issues.FriendlyName: Informatica RR date format element has century-guessing logic that differs from Snowflake YY.
Issues.Description: THE INFORMATICA RR DATE FORMAT ELEMENT USES CENTURY-GUESSING LOGIC (E.G., 99 MAPS TO 1999, 25 MAPS TO 2025) WHILE SNOWFLAKE YY ALWAYS MAPS TWO-DIGIT YEARS TO THE CURRENT CENTURY (E.G., 99 MAPS TO 2099). THE FORMAT HAS BEEN CONVERTED FROM RR TO YY. REVIEW DATE VALUES TO ENSURE CORRECT CENTURY INTERPRETATION.

Issues.FriendlyName: Informatica PowerCenter worklet contains user-defined variables.
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA POWERCENTER WORKLET CONTAINS USER-DEFINED VARIABLES THAT ARE NOT CONVERTED TO SNOWFLAKE

Issues.FriendlyName: Informatica POWER rounds non-integer exponents when the base is negative; Snowflake raises an error.
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA POWER(NEGATIVE_BASE, NON_INTEGER_EXPONENT) ROUNDS THE EXPONENT TO THE NEAREST INTEGER BEFORE COMPUTING. SNOWFLAKE POWER RAISES 'INVALID FLOATING POINT OPERATION' FOR NEGATIVE BASE WITH NON-INTEGER EXPONENT. REVIEW EXPRESSIONS USING POWER WITH POTENTIALLY NEGATIVE BASES AND NON-INTEGER EXPONENTS.

Issues.FriendlyName: Informatica MD5 may produce different hashes due to encoding differences
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA ALLOWS CONFIGURABLE CHARACTER ENCODING VIA THE DATAMOVEMENTMODE PROPERTY (ASCII OR UNICODE), WHILE SNOWFLAKE ALWAYS USES UTF-8. DIFFERENT ENCODINGS CAN PRODUCE DIFFERENT MD5 HASHES FOR THE SAME INPUT STRING.

Issues.FriendlyName: Informatica PowerCenter disconnected stored procedure call cannot be converted to Snowflake SQL.
Issues.Description: INFORMATICA POWERCENTER DISCONNECTED STORED PROCEDURE CALL ':SP.{0}' CANNOT BE CONVERTED TO A SQL EXPRESSION

Issues.FriendlyName: SETMAXVARIABLE/SETMINVARIABLE per-row return value on OUTPUT port may differ from Informatica.
Issues.Description: SETMAXVARIABLE/SETMINVARIABLE ON AN OUTPUT PORT: INFORMATICA ACCUMULATES THE VARIABLE ROW-BY-ROW, SO ROWS AFTER A NEW MAX/MIN REFLECT THE UPDATED VALUE. SNOWFLAKE COMPARES ALL ROWS AGAINST THE SAME INITIAL VALUE. PER-ROW RETURN VALUES MAY DIFFER. THE FINAL PERSISTED VARIABLE VALUE IS EQUIVALENT.

Issues.FriendlyName: SETVARIABLE persisted value may differ when row order matters.
Issues.Description: SETVARIABLE PERSISTS THE LAST ROW'S VALUE IN INFORMATICA (ORDER-DEPENDENT). SNOWFLAKE USES MAX/MIN ACROSS ALL ROWS INSTEAD. REVIEW IF ROW ORDER AFFECTS THE EXPECTED RESULT.

Issues.FriendlyName: SETVARIABLE with AGGFUNCTION=COUNT translated to COUNT(port).
Issues.Description: SETVARIABLE WITH AGGFUNCTION=COUNT IS TRANSLATED TO COUNT(PORT). INFORMATICA COUNTS ROW-BY-ROW INVOCATIONS, SO IF THE SETVARIABLE EXPRESSION IS CONDITIONAL, RESULTS MAY DIFFER.

Issues.FriendlyName: Stored procedure hook uses a named connection.
Issues.Description: STORED PROCEDURE HOOK USES NAMED CONNECTION '{0}'. VERIFY THE PROCEDURE EXISTS ON THE SNOWFLAKE TARGET CONNECTION.

Issues.FriendlyName: dbt folder hooks execute once per model, not once per session.
Issues.Description: DBT FOLDER HOOKS EXECUTE ONCE PER MODEL IN THE FOLDER, NOT ONCE PER SESSION AS IN INFORMATICA. IF THIS PROCEDURE MUST RUN ONLY ONCE, CONSIDER USING ON-RUN-START/ON-RUN-END INSTEAD.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS component is not supported by SnowConvert.
Issues.Description: SSIS COMPONENT IS NOT SUPPORTED BY SNOWCONVERT

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS expression cannot be converted to Snowflake SQL: {0}
Issues.Description: SSIS EXPRESSION CANNOT BE CONVERTED TO SNOWFLAKE SQL: {0}

Issues.FriendlyName: Embedded SQL cannot be converted
Issues.Description: EMBEDDED SQL CANNOT BE CONVERTED FROM {0} TO SNOWFLAKE SQL

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Control Flow Element cannot be converted.
Issues.Description: SSIS CONTROL FLOW ELEMENT {0} CANNOT BE CONVERTED TO SNOWFLAKE SCRIPTING.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS ExecutePackageTask was converted to a Snowflake EXECUTE TASK statement that runs asynchronously.
Issues.Description: SSIS EXECUTE PACKAGE TASK WAS CONVERTED TO A SNOWFLAKE EXECUTE TASK STATEMENT THAT RUNS ASYNCHRONOUSLY.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Execute Package Task variable bindings were not converted.
Issues.Description: SSIS EXECUTE PACKAGE TASK VARIABLE BINDINGS WERE NOT CONVERTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Executable contains property expressions that were not converted.
Issues.Description: SSIS EXECUTABLE CONTAINS PROPERTY EXPRESSIONS that were not converted.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask file attachments are not supported in Snowflake
Issues.Description: SNOWFLAKE'S SYSTEM$SEND_EMAIL DOES NOT SUPPORT FILE ATTACHMENTS. CONSIDER USING STAGED FILES WITH SHARED LINKS OR ALTERNATIVE DELIVERY METHODS.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask email priority is not supported in Snowflake
Issues.Description: EMAIL PRIORITY SETTINGS (HIGH/NORMAL/LOW) ARE NOT SUPPORTED BY SYSTEM$SEND_EMAIL AND WILL BE IGNORED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask file-based message source is not supported
Issues.Description: LOADING EMAIL BODY FROM A FILE CONNECTION IS NOT SUPPORTED. THE MESSAGE SOURCE MUST BE DIRECT INPUT OR FROM A VARIABLE.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask HTML body format is not supported in Snowflake
Issues.Description: SYSTEM$SEND_EMAIL ONLY SUPPORTS PLAIN TEXT EMAIL BODIES. HTML FORMATTING WILL NOT BE PRESERVED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Character Map operation '{0}' is not supported in Snowflake.
Issues.Description: SSIS CHARACTER MAP OPERATION '{0}' (MAPFLAGS={1}) IS NOT SUPPORTED IN SNOWFLAKE. ONLY UPPERCASE (512) AND LOWERCASE (256) OPERATIONS ARE TRANSLATED. THIS COLUMN PASSES THROUGH UNCHANGED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask SMTP connection settings are managed by Snowflake
Issues.Description: CUSTOM SMTP SERVER SETTINGS ARE NOT APPLICABLE. SNOWFLAKE MANAGES EMAIL DELIVERY THROUGH THE NOTIFICATION INTEGRATION.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask FROM address added to email body
Issues.Description: SNOWFLAKE'S EMAIL INTEGRATION USES A FIXED SENDER ADDRESS. THE ORIGINAL FROM ADDRESS HAS BEEN PREPENDED TO THE MESSAGE BODY FOR REFERENCE.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask CC addresses added to recipients list
Issues.Description: SNOWFLAKE'S SYSTEM$SEND_EMAIL DOES NOT SUPPORT CC ADDRESSING. ALL CC RECIPIENTS HAVE BEEN ADDED TO THE MAIN RECIPIENTS LIST.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS SendMailTask BCC addresses added to recipients list
Issues.Description: SNOWFLAKE'S SYSTEM$SEND_EMAIL DOES NOT SUPPORT BCC ADDRESSING. ALL BCC RECIPIENTS HAVE BEEN ADDED TO THE MAIN RECIPIENTS LIST, MAKING THEM VISIBLE TO ALL RECIPIENTS.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask native data file type not supported
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK NATIVE OR WIDENATIVE DATA FILE TYPE IS NOT SUPPORTED IN SNOWFLAKE. EXPORT SOURCE DATA TO CSV FORMAT BEFORE MIGRATION.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask LastRow option not supported
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK LASTROW OPTION IS NOT SUPPORTED IN SNOWFLAKE. USE TEMPORARY TABLE WITH ROW_NUMBER AND LIMIT/OFFSET TO SELECT SPECIFIC ROW RANGE.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask MaximumErrors semantic difference
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK MAXIMUMERRORS SPECIFIES ERROR COUNT THRESHOLD. SNOWFLAKE ON_ERROR CONTROLS BEHAVIOR WHEN ERRORS OCCUR. REVIEW ERROR HANDLING STRATEGY.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask BatchSize automatic management
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK BATCHSIZE IS NOT AVAILABLE IN SNOWFLAKE. SNOWFLAKE MANAGES BATCHING AUTOMATICALLY FOR OPTIMAL PERFORMANCE.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask identity auto-generation not supported
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK KEEPIDENTITY DISABLED. SNOWFLAKE COPY INTO WOULD TRY TO LOAD FILE VALUES INTO AUTOINCREMENT COLUMNS, WHICH MAY CAUSE ERRORS. EXCLUDE IDENTITY COLUMNS FROM THE FILE OR MODIFY THE TARGET TABLE.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask TableLock not needed
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK TABLELOCK IS NOT NEEDED IN SNOWFLAKE. MVCC ARCHITECTURE ALLOWS CONCURRENT READS DURING WRITES WITHOUT EXPLICIT LOCKING.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask SortedData hint not available
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK SORTEDDATA HINT IS NOT AVAILABLE IN SNOWFLAKE. USE CLUSTER BY ON TABLE DEFINITION FOR SIMILAR OPTIMIZATION.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask CheckConstraints always enforced
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK CHECKCONSTRAINTS OPTION IS IMPLICIT IN SNOWFLAKE. CONSTRAINTS ARE ALWAYS VALIDATED DURING DATA LOADING.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask field terminator not specified
Issues.Description: FIELD TERMINATOR NOT SPECIFIED IN DTSX. USING SSIS DEFAULT VALUE '{0}'. VERIFY THIS MATCHES YOUR DATA FILE FORMAT.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask row terminator not specified
Issues.Description: ROW TERMINATOR NOT SPECIFIED IN DTSX. USING SSIS DEFAULT VALUE '{0}'. VERIFY THIS MATCHES YOUR DATA FILE FORMAT.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask FireTriggers not supported
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK FIRETRIGGERS OPTION IS NOT SUPPORTED IN SNOWFLAKE. CONSIDER USING SNOWFLAKE STREAMS AND TASKS TO IMPLEMENT TRIGGER-LIKE BEHAVIOR.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask FormatFile not supported
Issues.Description: SSIS BULKINSERTTASK FORMAT FILE IS NOT SUPPORTED IN SNOWFLAKE. CREATE EQUIVALENT FILE FORMAT OBJECT MANUALLY BASED ON FORMAT FILE CONTENTS.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS BulkInsertTask stage and file upload not included
Issues.Description: THE STAGE AND FILE UPLOAD ARE NOT INCLUDED IN THE TRANSLATION. CREATE A SNOWFLAKE STAGE AND UPLOAD THE SOURCE FILE BEFORE EXECUTING THE COPY INTO STATEMENT. REPLACE {STAGE_PLACEHOLDER} WITH YOUR STAGE NAME.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Execute Package Task references an external package.
Issues.Description: EXECUTE PACKAGE TASK REFERENCES AN EXTERNAL PACKAGE

Issues.FriendlyName: Replace NULL with appropriate ORDER BY column(s) to ensure deterministic first match selection. SSIS lookup returns the first matching row, so proper ordering is required when multiple rows match the join condition.
Issues.Description: REPLACE NULL WITH APPROPRIATE ORDER BY COLUMN(S) TO ENSURE DETERMINISTIC FIRST MATCH SELECTION. SSIS LOOKUP RETURNS THE FIRST MATCHING ROW, SO PROPER ORDERING IS REQUIRED WHEN MULTIPLE ROWS MATCH THE JOIN CONDITION.

Issues.FriendlyName: Unexpected exception converting component.
Issues.Description: UNEXPECTED EXCEPTION CONVERTING COMPONENT.

Issues.FriendlyName: A model associated with this table is already defined.
Issues.Description: A model associated with table '{0}' is already defined.

Issues.FriendlyName: Result binding is configured for non-query statement.
Issues.Description: RESULT BINDING IS CONFIGURED FOR NON-QUERY STATEMENT. RESULT BINDING ONLY WORKS WITH SELECT QUERIES.

Issues.FriendlyName: XML result set type is not supported.
Issues.Description: XML RESULT SET TYPE IS NOT SUPPORTED. ONLY SINGLEROW AND FULLRESULTSET RESULT SET TYPES ARE SUPPORTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Property expression contains unsupported patterns.
Issues.Description: PROPERTY EXPRESSION FOR SQLSTATEMENTSOURCE CONTAINS UNSUPPORTED PATTERNS. ONLY SINGLE VARIABLE REFERENCES OR SIMPLE STRING CONCATENATION WITH LITERALS AND VARIABLE REFERENCES IS SUPPORTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: ForEach File Enumerator stage mapping required.
Issues.Description: THE FOLDER PATH REQUIRES MANUAL MAPPING TO A SNOWFLAKE STAGE.

Issues.FriendlyName: Add an ORDER BY clause to ensure sorted output. SSIS Merge requires sorted inputs and produces a sorted output.
Issues.Description: ADD AN ORDER BY CLAUSE TO ENSURE SORTED OUTPUT. THE SSIS MERGE TRANSFORMATION ASSUMES SORTED INPUTS AND NATURALLY PRODUCES A SORTED, DETERMINISTIC OUTPUT. THE EQUIVALENT SQL UNION ALL DOES NOT GUARANTEE ORDER.

Issues.FriendlyName: The SSIS container was converted inline.
Issues.Description: THE SSIS '{0}' CONTAINER WAS CONVERTED INLINE. Original container name: {1}

Issues.FriendlyName: Add an ORDER BY clause to ensure sorted output. SSIS Merge Join requires sorted inputs and produces a sorted output.
Issues.Description: ADD AN ORDER BY CLAUSE TO ENSURE SORTED OUTPUT. THE SSIS MERGE JOIN TRANSFORMATION ASSUMES SORTED INPUTS AND NATURALLY PRODUCES A SORTED, DETERMINISTIC OUTPUT. THE EQUIVALENT SQL JOIN DOES NOT GUARANTEE ORDER.

Issues.FriendlyName: Package was converted to stored procedure because it is being reused by other packages.
Issues.Description: PACKAGE WAS CONVERTED TO STORED PROCEDURE BECAUSE IT IS BEING REUSED BY OTHER PACKAGES.

Issues.FriendlyName: Event handler stored procedure created but not automatically triggered.
Issues.Description: EVENT HANDLER STORED PROCEDURE CREATED BUT NOT AUTOMATICALLY TRIGGERED. MANUAL INVOCATION OR TRIGGERING MECHANISM IMPLEMENTATION REQUIRED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Flat File Source stage path variable requires manual mapping to a Snowflake stage.
Issues.Description: FLAT FILE SOURCE STAGE PATH VARIABLE '{0}' REQUIRES MANUAL MAPPING TO A SNOWFLAKE STAGE.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS property expression is not supported.
Issues.Description: PROPERTY EXPRESSION '{0}' IS NOT SUPPORTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Flat File Source connection manager not found.
Issues.Description: FLAT FILE SOURCE CONNECTION MANAGER NOT FOUND.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source file staging required.
Issues.Description: SNOWFLAKE REQUIRES EXCEL FILES TO BE STAGED BEFORE PROCESSING. THE GENERATED CODE REFERENCES STAGE FILE PATH '{0}'. USE PUT COMMAND, SNOWSQL, SNOWSIGHT, OR OPENFLOW FOR FILE INGESTION.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Pivot PassThroughUnmatchedPivotKeys behavioral difference.
Issues.Description: SSIS PIVOT WITH PASSTHROUGHUNMATCHEDPIVOTKEYS ENABLED PASSES UNMATCHED PIVOT KEY ROWS TO A SEPARATE OUTPUT. THE CONVERTED SQL PRODUCES NULL VALUES FOR UNMATCHED PIVOT KEYS INSTEAD.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source error row redirection requires alternative implementation.
Issues.Description: SSIS ERROR OUTPUT WITH REDIRECTROW DISPOSITION CANNOT BE DIRECTLY TRANSLATED. USE TRY_TO_* FUNCTIONS WITH ERROR FLAG COLUMNS FOR DEFENSIVE ERROR HANDLING. CREATE SEPARATE ERROR CAPTURE MODELS IF NEEDED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source parameterized queries require dbt vars or macros.
Issues.Description: SSIS PARAMETERMAPPING PROPERTY '{0}' REQUIRES CONVERSION TO DBT VARS OR JINJA TEMPLATING. REPLACE SSIS PARAMETERS WITH DBT VARS AND PASS VALUES AT RUNTIME USING --VARS.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source project-level connection manager requires external resolution.
Issues.Description: CONNECTION MANAGER '{0}' IS DEFINED AT PROJECT LEVEL (IN .CONMGR FILE), NOT IN THE .DTSX PACKAGE. LOCATE THE CORRESPONDING .CONMGR FILE AND EXTRACT CONNECTIONSTRING FOR EXCEL FILE PATH AND HDR SETTING.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source legacy format may have behavioral differences.
Issues.Description: LEGACY .XLS OR BINARY .XLSB FILE '{0}' MAY HAVE MINOR DIFFERENCES IN DATE/NUMBER HANDLING COMPARED TO .XLSX. THE UDF AUTO-DETECTS FORMAT FROM FILENAME. FOR DATE COLUMNS, CONSIDER USING DATEADD SERIAL CONVERSION PATTERN.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source variable-based access mode not supported.
Issues.Description: EXCEL SOURCE ACCESS MODE {0} USES SSIS VARIABLE '{1}' TO RESOLVE THE SHEET NAME OR SQL QUERY AT RUNTIME. VARIABLE-BASED ACCESS MODES ARE NOT TRANSLATED. THE GENERATED MODEL READS THE FULL SHEET USING THE STATIC OPENROWSET VALUE. MANUALLY ADD THE DYNAMIC SHEET/QUERY LOGIC USING DBT VARS.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source SQL command filtering logic not preserved.
Issues.Description: EXCEL SOURCE USES SQL COMMAND ACCESS MODE WITH QUERY: '{0}'. THE GENERATED MODEL READS THE FULL SHEET VIA UDF WITHOUT APPLYING THE ORIGINAL SQL FILTERING, JOINS, OR AGGREGATION LOGIC. ADD THE EQUIVALENT FILTERING AS DOWNSTREAM CTES IN THE DBT MODEL.

Issues.FriendlyName: Excel Source CONFIG variable requires manual mapping.
Issues.Description: THE GENERATED ORCHESTRATION DEFINES CONFIG VARIABLE '{0}' (STAGE FILE PATH) WITH DEFAULT VALUE FROM THE ORIGINAL SSIS PACKAGE. UPDATE THIS VARIABLE IN THE TASK CONFIG SECTION TO POINT TO YOUR SNOWFLAKE STAGE AND EXCEL FILE PATH (E.G., @MY_STAGE/FILE.XLSX) BEFORE EXECUTION.

Issues.FriendlyName: Flat file format is not supported.
Issues.Description: FLAT FILE FORMAT '{0}' IS NOT SUPPORTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Non-standard header row delimiter is not supported.
Issues.Description: FLAT FILE HEADER ROW DELIMITER IS NOT A NEWLINE CHARACTER. THIS IS NOT SUPPORTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: Flat File Source feature is not supported.
Issues.Description: {0} IS NOT SUPPORTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Pivot assumes input data is sorted by Set Key.
Issues.Description: THE SSIS PIVOT TRANSFORMATION ASSUMES INPUT DATA IS SORTED BY THE SET KEY COLUMN. THE CONVERTED SQL USES GROUP BY WHICH DOES NOT REQUIRE OR PRESERVE SORT ORDER. VERIFY THAT DOWNSTREAM CONSUMERS DO NOT DEPEND ON SORTED OUTPUT.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FuzzyLookup uses JAROWINKLER_SIMILARITY which approximates token-based matching.
Issues.Description: THE SSIS FUZZYLOOKUP TRANSFORMATION USES A TOKEN-BASED SIMILARITY ALGORITHM. THE CONVERTED SQL USES JAROWINKLER_SIMILARITY WHICH IS A CHARACTER-LEVEL ALGORITHM. SIMILARITY SCORES MAY DIFFER BETWEEN SOURCE AND TARGET. _CONFIDENCE IS APPROXIMATED AS _SIMILARITY (NO EQUIVALENT RELATIVE CONFIDENCE METRIC IN SNOWFLAKE). UNMATCHED INPUT ROWS ARE EXCLUDED — SSIS PRESERVES ALL INPUT ROWS WITH SIMILARITY=0 AND NULL REFERENCE COLUMNS.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FuzzyLookup multi-column fuzzy matching not supported.
Issues.Description: THE SSIS FUZZYLOOKUP TRANSFORMATION HAS MULTIPLE FUZZY JOIN COLUMNS. ONLY THE FIRST FUZZY COLUMN IS USED FOR SIMILARITY SCORING. MULTI-COLUMN AVERAGING IS NOT YET IMPLEMENTED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FuzzyLookup exact join columns not supported.
Issues.Description: THE SSIS FUZZYLOOKUP TRANSFORMATION HAS EXACT JOIN COLUMNS (JOINTYPE=1). EXACT JOIN PRE-FILTERING IS NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — THE GENERATED SQL USES A FULL CROSS JOIN INSTEAD.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS expression function not reviewed for Snowflake equivalence
Issues.Description: SSIS EXPRESSION FUNCTION '{0}' HAS NOT BEEN REVIEWED FOR SNOWFLAKE EQUIVALENCE

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS expression circular variable reference detected
Issues.Description: CIRCULAR REFERENCE DETECTED WHEN EXPANDING VARIABLE '{0}'. THE DEFAULT VALUE FOR TYPE '{1}' HAS BEEN USED INSTEAD.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask directory path translated to Snowflake stage prefix
Issues.Description: SNOWFLAKE STAGES USE PREFIX-BASED PATHS, NOT REAL DIRECTORIES. THE REMOVE COMMAND WITH A TRAILING SLASH DELETES ALL FILES MATCHING THE PREFIX PATTERN.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask variable must contain a valid Snowflake stage path
Issues.Description: THE VARIABLE(S) VALUE(S) MUST CONTAIN A VALID SNOWFLAKE STAGE PATH.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask CreateDirectory translated using COPY INTO placeholder file
Issues.Description: SINCE SNOWFLAKE STAGES USE PREFIX-BASED PATHS, THE DIRECTORY IS CREATED BY WRITING A .DUMMY PLACEHOLDER FILE TO THE TARGET PATH VIA COPY INTO.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask path requires manual mapping to a Snowflake stage
Issues.Description: THE PATH(S) REQUIRES MANUAL MAPPING TO A SNOWFLAKE STAGE(S).

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask overwrite=false not supported in Snowflake
Issues.Description: THE ORIGINAL SSIS TASK WAS CONFIGURED TO FAIL IF THE DESTINATION EXISTS. SNOWFLAKE DOES NOT SUPPORT THIS BEHAVIOR AND WILL SILENTLY OVERWRITE.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask references an external or unresolvable connection manager
Issues.Description: THE CONNECTION MANAGER REFERENCED BY TASKSOURCEPATH OR TASKDESTINATIONPATH IS NOT DEFINED IN THE DTSX FILE. IT MAY BE A PROJECT-LEVEL CONNECTION MANAGER. THE PATH CANNOT BE RESOLVED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask SetAttributes operation not supported in Snowflake
Issues.Description: SNOWFLAKE STAGES DO NOT SUPPORT FILE ATTRIBUTES (READ-ONLY, HIDDEN, ETC.). THIS OPERATION CANNOT BE TRANSLATED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS FileSystemTask COPY FILES destination appends source filename
Issues.Description: THE COPY FILES COMMAND TREATS THE DESTINATION AS A DIRECTORY PREFIX AND APPENDS THE SOURCE FILENAME. IF THE DESTINATION PATH REFERS TO A SPECIFIC FILE RATHER THAN A DIRECTORY, THE RESULTING PATH MAY BE INCORRECT. REVIEW AND ADJUST MANUALLY IF NEEDED.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Character Map new-column mode (usageType=readOnly) is not supported.
Issues.Description: SSIS CHARACTER MAP COLUMN '{0}' USES NEW-COLUMN MODE (USAGETYPE=READONLY). ONLY IN-PLACE MODE (USAGETYPE=READWRITE) IS TRANSLATED. THE ORIGINAL COLUMN PASSES THROUGH UNCHANGED WITHOUT THE CHARACTER MAP OPERATION.

Issues.FriendlyName: SSIS Cache Transform duplicate key validation behavior difference
Issues.Description: SSIS CACHE TRANSFORM WITH TREATDUPLICATEKEYSASERROR=TRUE ENFORCES DUPLICATE KEY VALIDATION AT RUNTIME. IN THE DBT TRANSLATION, THIS VALIDATION IS NOT ENFORCED AND DUPLICATE ROWS WILL PASS THROUGH UNCHANGED. THE TRANSLATED SQL WORKS CORRECTLY AS A PASSTHROUGH. IF DUPLICATE KEY VALIDATION IS REQUIRED FOR DATA QUALITY, CONSIDER ADDING A DBT TEST OR UNIQUE CONSTRAINT.
